testResults.name: SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService loads ancestor chains for multiple roots with one parent_id-only recursive query
SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService loads direct children for multiple parents with one parent_id batch query
SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService projects a descendant tree with cursor pagination and max depth using one recursive query
SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService uses the paginated tree projection for descendant traversal
SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService summarizes direct and subtree rollup counts with explicit percent denominator rules
SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService flags stale direct-child projections when expected rollup counts disagree with storage
SqliteWorkGraphContainmentQueryService groups role-filtered ready frontier tasks and separates dependency blockers from gate blockers
validateWorkGraphHierarchy accepts the canonical saga -> epic -> task -> subtask hierarchy shape
validateWorkGraphHierarchy rejects saga parent_id so Saga membership stays relation-backed
validateWorkGraphHierarchy rejects epic parents, task-under-task, and child-under-subtask matrix breaches
validateWorkGraphHierarchy can throw with stable diagnostics for callers that need fail-fast enforcement
SqliteWorkGraphRelationQueryService lists outgoing task_relations with stored reasons and without dependencies by default
SqliteWorkGraphRelationQueryService lists incoming relation edges and maps null reasons to undefined
SqliteWorkGraphRelationQueryService defaults to both directions and preserves advisory relation semantics
SqliteWorkGraphRelationQueryService keeps dependencies opt-in and tags them separately from relation rows
validateWorkGraphRelationQuality flags groups-as-hierarchy misuse, missing reasons, and depends-vs-relates ambiguity
validateWorkGraphRelationQuality flags groups-as-hierarchy in either direction across parent_id containment
validateWorkGraphRelationQuality accepts reasoned advisory relations that stay distinct from containment and dependencies
preflightWorkGraphReparent rejects moves that would create a parent_id containment cycle
preflightWorkGraphReparent rejects moves that would exceed the configured containment depth
preflightWorkGraphReparent returns typed findings while allowing structurally safe reparent proposals
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC1 — apply is transactional returns applied:false when validation fails (invalid type)
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC1 — apply is transactional returns applied:false when apply flag is false (dry-run preview)
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC1 — apply is transactional returns applied:true only when all node and edge writes succeed
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC2 — idempotency prevents duplicates skips nodes that already exist
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC2 — idempotency prevents duplicates edges are idempotent — duplicate depends_on does not throw
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC2 — idempotency prevents duplicates relation edges are idempotent — duplicate blocks does not throw
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC3 — docs and relations created consistently depends_on edge creates a task_dependencies row
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC3 — docs and relations created consistently blocks edge creates a task_relations row
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC3 — docs and relations created consistently relates_to edge creates a task_relations row
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC3 — docs and relations created consistently groups edge creates a task_relations row
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC3 — docs and relations created consistently parentId on node sets task parent_id (containment)
WorkGraph scaffold apply engine AC3 — docs and relations created consistently mixed nodes + edges: all three ACs exercised end-to-end
AC1: scaffold validates saga/epic/task/subtask nodes and edges accepts a valid saga with epics and tasks
AC1: scaffold validates saga/epic/task/subtask nodes and edges accepts a valid epic→task→subtask hierarchy
AC1: scaffold validates saga/epic/task/subtask nodes and edges accepts edges between valid nodes
AC1: scaffold validates saga/epic/task/subtask nodes and edges rejects a task whose parent is a subtask (hierarchy violation)
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects nodes with empty or missing IDs
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects duplicate node IDs
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects nodes with invalid types
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects edges referencing non-existent source nodes
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects edges referencing non-existent target nodes
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects self-loop edges
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks rejects edges with invalid kind
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks flags fanout exceeding the configured maxFanout
AC2: IDs, edges, and fanout checks does not flag fanout at or under the configured limit
AC3: dry-run default is true defaults dryRun to true when not specified
AC3: dry-run default is true echoes explicit dryRun: true
AC3: dry-run default is true echoes explicit dryRun: false
edge cases accepts empty nodes array (root-only scope)
edge cases accepts undefined edges
edge cases accepts all four valid types
edge cases returns multiple issues when scaffold has several problems
validateWorkGraphStructure returns typed cycle, orphan, depth, fanout, and missing Wave 0 findings
validateWorkGraphStructure paginates large structural finding sets with stable next cursors
